title: DSDS Variants Document Block
description: Documents all dimensions of visual or behavioral variation on a component. Each item is either a flag variant (a boolean toggle such as 'disabled' or 'full-width') or an enum variant (a set of mutually exclusive options such as size: sm | md | lg). Every variant can carry a rationale that explains why the variation exists.

variantValue.description: A single option within an enum variant dimension.

description.description: What this value looks like, when to use it, and how it differs from other values in this dimension.

rationale.description: Why this value exists: the user need or design rationale it addresses (e.g., 'High emphasis directs the user to the single most important action on the surface'). For comparative when-to-use guidance across values, use the entity's useCases block instead.

examples.description: Examples showing this variant value in isolation or in context.

tokens.description: Design token overrides applied when this value is selected. Keys are token-purpose names (e.g., 'background', 'text-color', 'padding-horizontal'). Values are token identifiers. In a system with a token layer these MUST be token identifiers, not raw values, so the value can't silently drift from the token source.

flagVariant.description: A boolean toggle variant that is either on or off. Use it for binary capabilities such as 'disabled', 'full-width', 'icon-only', or 'loading'. Flag variants have no values array. The component either has the flag active or not.

kind.description: Identifies this variant as a boolean flag.

name.description: Human-readable name of the flag (e.g., 'Disabled', 'Full Width', 'Icon Only', 'Loading').

description.description: What this flag controls and how it affects the component's appearance or behavior when active.

rationale.description: Why this flag exists: the user need or design rationale it addresses (e.g., 'Prevents user interaction during form submission to avoid duplicate requests').

examples.description: Examples showing the component with this flag active.

tokens.description: Design token overrides applied when this flag is active. Keys are token-purpose names. Values are token identifiers. In a system with a token layer these MUST be token identifiers, not raw values, so the value can't silently drift from the token source.

enumVariant.description: An enumerated variant with multiple mutually exclusive values. Use it for dimensions where the user picks one option from a set, such as 'size' (sm | md | lg), 'emphasis' (primary | secondary | ghost), or 'shape' (rounded | pill | square).

kind.description: Identifies this variant as an enumerated set of values.

identifier.description: Machine-readable identifier of the variant dimension (e.g., 'size', 'emphasis', 'shape'). This is the axis name, not a value within it.

name.description: Human-readable name of the variant dimension (e.g., 'Size', 'Emphasis', 'Shape').

description.description: What this dimension of variation controls and how its values affect the component's appearance or behavior.

values.description: The possible values for this variant. Must contain at least two entries. Model a single-value dimension as a flag variant instead. Ordering is significant, so tools SHOULD keep it for display.

variants.description: Documents all dimensions of visual or behavioral variation on a component. Each item is either a flag variant (a boolean toggle like 'disabled' or 'full-width') or an enum variant (a set of mutually exclusive options like size: sm | md | lg). Multiple items document orthogonal dimensions that combine independently.

kind.description: Identifies this document block as a variants spec.

items.description: The variant dimensions. Each item is either a flag (boolean toggle) or an enum (set of values). Ordering is significant, so tools SHOULD display dimensions in this order.
